sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content:
I did some research with Gemini about DMGTLAS as a system:

What I should focus on when marketing:

  • Integrity, Verifiability, and Architectural Awareness

  • The World’s First Self-Healing, Architecturally-Aware Engineering Intelligence

  • "Axiom isn't just an AI that writes code; it’s a multi-agent ecosystem that maintains a living map of your entire software architecture. By triangulating between Business Intent (PRD), Technical Design (Spec), and Deterministic Code Structure (AST), Axiom ensures that every line of code is verifiable, every test is relevant, and your documentation is never out of date."

  • The Impact Guardrail: "Our GitHub Action generates a 'Semantic Impact Report.' Before you merge, you’ll know exactly which user guides, tests, and workflows were affected by a single function change."

  • Neuro-Symbolic Reliability: "We combine the creative reasoning of LLMs (Neural) with the rigid logic of AST Graphs (Symbolic). It’s the speed of AI with the safety of a Senior Architect."

  • "Most agents are just fast typists. Axiom is a Librarian, an Architect, and a Lead Engineer working in perfect sync. It is the first system where the documentation actually gets better the more the code changes."

The Components: Re-Imagined for the DMGLAS Guide

System

"Axiom" Name (Technical)

"Magical Guide" Name (Persona)

The "Magic" Behind It

dFlow

Workflow Documentation

The Star-Charts

Mapping the "Highways" of intent so the traveler never gets lost in the spaghetti.

Cortex

AST Parser

The Deep Roots

A deterministic skeleton that feels the pulse of every function and test, ensuring no "ghost" code remains.

DocJson

Documentation System

The Living Ledger

Not just words on a page, but a structured memory that grows and heals as the system evolves.

PEV Agent

The Executive

The Triumvirate

Three spirits: The Architect (Vision), The Builder (Action), and The Auditor (Balance).

Sub-System

Primary Role

The "Ground Truth"

Insight

Pitch

dFlow - Workflow Documentation System

The Orchestration Map

Orchestrator maps that define the "Highways" of the app logic.

Allows the user to lay a roadmap for core functionalities to allow deeper understanding of the "glue" that connects modules/functions together

"Stop guessing how data moves. dFlow maps the 'Highways' of your application logic, documenting the core orchestrators that drive your business value. It turns spaghetti code into a navigable architectural map."

Cortex - AST Parser

Structural "What"

A deterministic hierarchical graph of functions, classes, and test-to-code mappings back with staleness checking

Allows the user to get a hierarchical knowledge graph with various levels of details regarding functions, modules, and tests.

"The 'Ground Truth' for your codebase. Cortex uses high-precision AST parsing and staleness hashing to ensure your AI never navigates a 'ghost' version of your repo. It is the immutable link between your functions and your tests."

DocJson Atlas- Documentation System

Intentional "What, Why & How" - The Living Memory

Linkable and taggable json-native documents & document sections for referencing ideas and code

Whole documents and document sections can tagged and linked to code via Cortex and/or workflows via dFlow. This document format allows for users to query documents like a database for relevant documents/sections that pertain to a given task. Also can allow for making reports based of tags and links from other documents as well.

"The brain of the system. A JSON-native knowledge base that links atomic documentation sections directly to code nodes. It’s a human-readable, machine-queryable 'Why' for every 'What' in your system."

PEV Multi-Agent Nexus

Multi-Agent Governance Layer

An agentic reasoning system that navigates the other 3 systems to execute tasks

Given the other components, this system will have a map of the repository and will map decisions to Cortex node updates and use the staleness updates to ensure documentation is updated. Ca propose document/test updates based off

"A specialized trio of agents that follow the professional Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC). The Architect plans, the Builder implements with technical freedom, and the Auditor reconciles the two—proposing 'Knowledge Updates' to keep your docs in sync with reality."

sections.heading: PEV-Agent-System | Spec-Driven Development
sections.content:
1. The Architect (Planning Agent)

  • Role: Requirements gathering and "Ground Truth" alignment.

  • Input: User Request + Cortex (to see what exists) + DocJson (to see why).

  • Output: A Design Spec and a Task List, User guide, and PRD.

  • The Logic: This agent doesn't write code. It writes the "Contract." By having the user verify the PRD (Product Requirements Document) and Design Spec before execution, you eliminate 90% of wasted compute. It ensures the "Highways" in dFlow are correctly mapped before a single line is changed.

2. The Builder (Execution Agent)

  • Role: High-precision coding.

  • Input: Verified Design Spec + Targeted Cortex nodes (the specific functions to change).

  • Output: Code Diff, assumptions made out-of-scope, and implementation Notes.

  • The Logic: This agent is "blind" to the big picture—it just follows the Architect's blueprints. This makes it much more reliable because its context window isn't cluttered with "Why" questions; it only cares about the "How."

3. The Auditor (Validation Agent)

  • Role: Integrity and "Knowledge Sync."

  • Input: Original Code + New Code + Cortex Staleness Report + DocJson.

  • Output: Test Updates, Doc Updates, and an Impact Report.

Benefits:

  • Instead of a loose Markdown file that an agent might misinterpret, you’re providing a Structured Contract where every requirement is pinned to a specific Cortex_Node_ID or a dFlow_Path

  • Multi-Agent Governance Layer to maintain the integrity of your entire knowledge base.

Example workflow (AI-generated):
1. The "Soft" Placeholder as an Intent Contract

Instead of a rigid "you must build this exact function," the Architect places a node in DocJson that acts as a Functional Requirement.

  • Architect's Instruction: "I need a way to persist these models. I suggest using SQLModel because it's already in our stack."

  • The Builder's Freedom: The Builder is instructed: "Your priority is the Objective. If the suggested tool or structure is technically flawed, pivot and document why."

2. The Builder's "Implementation Manifest"
Builder Prompt:

Role: Execution Engineer
North Star (PRD): DocJson:Project_X_PRD (The "What" and "Why")
Current Map (Design Spec): DocJson:Spec_Node_42 (The "How")
Constraint: If the Map (Spec) contradicts the North Star (PRD) due to technical limitations in the libraries found in Cortex, you MUST pivot.
Deliverable: Code + Assumption Log (Explain every deviation from the Spec).

To maintain Separation of Concerns, the Builder shouldn't be the one updating the official Cortex nodes or the DocJson guides. Its only job is to provide the Implementation Manifest alongside the code.

The Builder's Output (JSON):

JSON

{
  "task_id": "TSK-402",
  "objective_met": true,
  "deviations": [
    {
      "original_plan": "Use SQLModel for UserProfile",
      "actual_implementation": "Used SQLAlchemy Core",
      "reason": "SQLModel's async session handling caused a race condition in the existing middleware found in Cortex:main.py",
      "impact": "Requires update to DocJson:Database_Setup_Guide"
    }
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "Assumed the environment variable 'DB_URL' is handled by the Docker sidecar."
  ]
}

3. The Auditor as the "Map Reconciler"

This is the most critical role. The Auditor receives the Builder's Manifest and the new code. Its concern is Consistency.

  1. Detects the Pivot: The Auditor reads: "I used SQLAlchemy instead of SQLModel."

  2. Analyzes the Impact: It checks Cortex and dFlow to see if this change "broke" the map.

  3. Proposes the Fix: It generates a Knowledge Update PR.

    • "The Builder pivoted to SQLAlchemy for technical reasons. I have updated the Design Spec in DocJson and the dFlow Highway to reflect this. Please approve."
